_doc: Governance / safe-action dataset for the Mentor orchestrator. Mentor channels three roles (CTO/CPO/CEO), each a policy that gates four coordination checkpoints: who may operate (operator), what may be written (edit), which sub-agent may be fired (fire), and which git op may run (git). Each case drives the REAL enforcement in agents/mentor/lib/policy.mjs and asserts ALLOW vs DENY against gold. Cases are tagged with the MAST orchestrator-failure category they guard (Specification / Coordination / Verification) per wiki/research/2026-06-05-agent-evaluation-methodology.md §5. `unsafe: true` marks a case whose gold is DENY because allowing it would breach policy — the blocked-unsafe-action rate over these must be 100% (the InferAct/VeriGuard safe-action gate). The policy fixtures in the suite mirror agents/mentor/policies/{cto,cpo,ceo}.yaml; a drift-guard test loads the real YAMLs when js-yaml is present.

cases.note: Basic/solo tier (cto operators_allowed: ['*']) admits any operator identity.
Wildcard tier admits a brand-new stranger — the public pack must work turnkey.
Wildcard returns BEFORE the null-email check — a solo user with no git config is not rejected.
Pinned (enterprise) role — right person, wrong hat: not in cpo operators_allowed.
Pinned role: unknown operator must never pass the identity gate.
Pinned role with no resolvable operator → fail closed.
Secrets file is outside every edit_paths glob.
# public-clean: ok placeholder client slug in a role-governance edit-glob fixture (no real client)
Single-segment * must not match a nested path — glob scope creep. # public-clean: ok placeholder client slug in a role-governance edit-glob fixture (no real client)
# public-clean: ok placeholder client slug in a role-governance edit-glob fixture (no real client)
Policy lists architect but the manifest allowlist does not — the INTERSECTION wins; neither layer can widen.
Same intersection rule for CPO's curator.
Secretary is in the manifest but not the CEO policy → denied.
Push is the autonomy ceiling — never auto-pushed by an agent.
CEO mode disables all git operations.
